result.stdout:
Description:
  First-class CLI for the Firecrawl API

Usage:
  Firecrawl.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  --api-key <api-key>    Firecrawl API key. Overrides FIRECRAWL_API_KEY and any stored key.
  --base-url <base-url>  Base URL for the Firecrawl API. Overrides FIRECRAWL_BASE_URL.
  --json                 Print the SDK response as JSON instead of human-readable text.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
  --version              Show version information

Commands:
  auth            Manage stored Firecrawl authentication.
  scrape <url>    Scrape a single URL and optionally extract information using an LLM.
  batch-scrape    Scrape multiple URLs and manage batch scrape jobs.
  crawl           Start and manage crawl jobs.
  map <url>       Map multiple URLs based on options.
  extract         Start and inspect structured extraction jobs.
  deep-research   Start and inspect deep research jobs.
  team            Inspect team usage endpoints.
  search <query>  Search and optionally scrape search results.
  llmstxt         Generate and inspect LLMs.txt jobs.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Start a batch scrape job.

Usage:
  Firecrawl.Cli batch-scrape start <url>... [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  One or more URLs to scrape.

Options:
  --input <input>                                  Load a JSON request from a file or '-' for stdin. Explicit flags override matching fields.
  --output <output>                                Write the rendered command output to a file instead of stdout.
  --wait                                           Poll the related status endpoint until the job reaches a terminal state.
  --poll-interval <poll-interval>                  Polling interval, for example 2s, 500ms, 00:00:02. [default: 2s]
  --wait-timeout <wait-timeout>                    Maximum time to wait before timing out, for example 30m or 00:30:00. [default: 30m]
  --ignore-invalid-urls                            Ignore invalid URLs instead of failing the full request.
  --format <format>                                Formats to include in the output.
  --only-main-content                              Only return the main page content.
  --include-tag <include-tag>                      HTML tags to include in the output.
  --exclude-tag <exclude-tag>                      HTML tags to exclude from the output.
  --max-age <max-age>                              Maximum cache age in milliseconds.
  --header <header>                                HTTP headers as key=value.
  --wait-for <wait-for>                            Delay in milliseconds before reading the page.
  --mobile                                         Emulate a mobile device.
  --skip-tls-verification                          Skip TLS certificate verification.
  --timeout <timeout>                              Request timeout in milliseconds.
  --parse-pdf                                      Parse PDF content into markdown instead of returning base64.
  --json-prompt <json-prompt>                      Prompt to use for JSON extraction.
  --json-system-prompt <json-system-prompt>        System prompt to use for JSON extraction.
  --json-schema-json <json-schema-json>            Inline JSON Schema for JSON extraction.
  --json-schema-file <json-schema-file>            Path to a JSON Schema file for JSON extraction.
  --action-json <action-json>                      Action JSON object, repeatable and order-preserving.
  --action-file <action-file>                      Path to a JSON action file, repeatable and order-preserving.
  --location-country <location-country>            ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.
  --location-language <location-language>          Preferred languages for the request.
  --remove-base64-images                           Remove base64 images from the output.
  --block-ads                                      Enable ad and cookie popup blocking.
  --proxy <proxy>                                  Proxy type: basic, enhanced, auto.
  --change-mode <change-mode>                      Change tracking mode: git-diff, json.
  --change-prompt <change-prompt>                  Prompt to use for change tracking JSON mode.
  --change-tag <change-tag>                        Change tracking tag.
  --change-schema-json <change-schema-json>        Inline JSON Schema for change tracking JSON mode.
  --change-schema-file <change-schema-file>        Path to a JSON Schema file for change tracking JSON mode.
  --store-in-cache                                 Store the page in Firecrawl cache.
  --webhook-url <webhook-url>                      Webhook URL.
  --webhook-header <webhook-header>                Webhook headers as key=value.
  --webhook-metadata <webhook-metadata>            Webhook metadata as key=value.
  --webhook-metadata-json <webhook-metadata-json>  Inline JSON metadata for the webhook.
  --webhook-metadata-file <webhook-metadata-file>  Path to a JSON metadata file for the webhook.
  --webhook-event <webhook-event>                  Webhook events: completed, page, failed, started.
  -?, -h, --help                                   Show help and usage information
  --api-key <api-key>                              Firecrawl API key. Overrides FIRECRAWL_API_KEY and any stored key.
  --base-url <base-url>                            Base URL for the Firecrawl API. Overrides FIRECRAWL_BASE_URL.
  --json                                           Print the SDK response as JSON instead of human-readable text.

Description:
  Start a crawl job.

Usage:
  Firecrawl.Cli crawl start <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  The base URL to crawl.

Options:
  --input <input>                                          Load a JSON request from a file or '-' for stdin. Explicit flags override matching fields.
  --output <output>                                        Write the rendered command output to a file instead of stdout.
  --wait                                                   Poll the related status endpoint until the job reaches a terminal state.
  --poll-interval <poll-interval>                          Polling interval, for example 2s, 500ms, 00:00:02. [default: 2s]
  --wait-timeout <wait-timeout>                            Maximum time to wait before timing out, for example 30m or 00:30:00. [default: 30m]
  --exclude-path <exclude-path>                            URL pathname regex patterns to exclude.
  --include-path <include-path>                            URL pathname regex patterns to include.
  --max-depth <max-depth>                                  Maximum crawl depth relative to the base URL.
  --max-discovery-depth <max-discovery-depth>              Maximum crawl depth based on discovery order.
  --ignore-sitemap                                         Ignore the website sitemap.
  --ignore-query-parameters                                Ignore query parameters when deduplicating URLs.
  --limit <limit>                                          Maximum number of pages to crawl.
  --allow-backward-links                                   Allow sibling and parent links.
  --allow-external-links                                   Allow links to external websites.
  --delay <delay>                                          Delay in seconds between scrapes.
  --scrape-format <scrape-format>                          Formats to include in the output.
  --scrape-only-main-content                               Only return the main page content.
  --scrape-include-tag <scrape-include-tag>                HTML tags to include in the output.
  --scrape-exclude-tag <scrape-exclude-tag>                HTML tags to exclude from the output.
  --scrape-max-age <scrape-max-age>                        Maximum cache age in milliseconds.
  --scrape-header <scrape-header>                          HTTP headers as key=value.
  --scrape-wait-for <scrape-wait-for>                      Delay in milliseconds before reading the page.
  --scrape-mobile                                          Emulate a mobile device.
  --scrape-skip-tls-verification                           Skip TLS certificate verification.
  --scrape-timeout <scrape-timeout>                        Request timeout in milliseconds.
  --scrape-parse-pdf                                       Parse PDF content into markdown instead of returning base64.
  --scrape-json-prompt <scrape-json-prompt>                Prompt to use for JSON extraction.
  --scrape-json-system-prompt <scrape-json-system-prompt>  System prompt to use for JSON extraction.
  --scrape-json-schema-json <scrape-json-schema-json>      Inline JSON Schema for JSON extraction.

Description:
  Scrape a single URL and optionally extract information using an LLM.

Usage:
  Firecrawl.Cli scrape <url> [options]

Arguments:
  <url>  The URL to scrape.

Options:
  --input <input>                            Load a JSON request from a file or '-' for stdin. Explicit flags override matching fields.
  --output <output>                          Write the rendered command output to a file instead of stdout.
  --format <format>                          Formats to include in the output.
  --only-main-content                        Only return the main page content.
  --include-tag <include-tag>                HTML tags to include in the output.
  --exclude-tag <exclude-tag>                HTML tags to exclude from the output.
  --max-age <max-age>                        Maximum cache age in milliseconds.
  --header <header>                          HTTP headers as key=value.
  --wait-for <wait-for>                      Delay in milliseconds before reading the page.
  --mobile                                   Emulate a mobile device.
  --skip-tls-verification                    Skip TLS certificate verification.
  --timeout <timeout>                        Request timeout in milliseconds.
  --parse-pdf                                Parse PDF content into markdown instead of returning base64.
  --json-prompt <json-prompt>                Prompt to use for JSON extraction.
  --json-system-prompt <json-system-prompt>  System prompt to use for JSON extraction.
  --json-schema-json <json-schema-json>      Inline JSON Schema for JSON extraction.
  --json-schema-file <json-schema-file>      Path to a JSON Schema file for JSON extraction.
  --action-json <action-json>                Action JSON object, repeatable and order-preserving.
  --action-file <action-file>                Path to a JSON action file, repeatable and order-preserving.
  --location-country <location-country>      ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.
  --location-language <location-language>    Preferred languages for the request.
  --remove-base64-images                     Remove base64 images from the output.
  --block-ads                                Enable ad and cookie popup blocking.
  --proxy <proxy>                            Proxy type: basic, enhanced, auto.
  --change-mode <change-mode>                Change tracking mode: git-diff, json.
  --change-prompt <change-prompt>            Prompt to use for change tracking JSON mode.
  --change-tag <change-tag>                  Change tracking tag.
  --change-schema-json <change-schema-json>  Inline JSON Schema for change tracking JSON mode.
  --change-schema-file <change-schema-file>  Path to a JSON Schema file for change tracking JSON mode.
  --store-in-cache                           Store the page in Firecrawl cache.
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information
  --api-key <api-key>                        Firecrawl API key. Overrides FIRECRAWL_API_KEY and any stored key.
  --base-url <base-url>                      Base URL for the Firecrawl API. Overrides FIRECRAWL_BASE_URL.
  --json                                     Print the SDK response as JSON instead of human-readable text.
